item.title: Re: Need help with translation
item.content:encoded: …The author is on hiatus, and isn't SCPDeclassified only for declassifications? I've come to r/SCP and people say I should go here.

item.title: Re: Need help with translation
item.content:encoded: Wept, u should PM the author or go to SCPD.

item.title: Need help with translation
item.content:encoded:
I am currently translating this article to Vietnamese, and I have no idea what "1st/2nd Revised Green Shooting Draft" means. I have googled and found out that in screenwriting, revisions of the shooting script are color coded, with "Green" being the 5th revision. But there's also "1st/2nd Revised", so I don't know how am I supposed to understand it. Can anyone explain to me?

Edit: I translated it anyways, so… here's one monstrosity of a translation.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Other licensing info:

Photo of Durand is public domain.

Aerial photo is by Los Alamos National Library, which requires the following to be stated:

Unless otherwise indicated, this information has been authored by an employee or employees of the Los Alamos National Security, LLC (LANS), operator of the Los Alamos National Laboratory under Contract No. DE-AC52-06NA25396 with the U.S. Department of Energy. The U.S. Government has rights to use, reproduce, and distribute this information. The public may copy and use this information without charge, provided that this Notice and any statement of authorship are reproduced on all copies. Neither the Government nor LANS makes any warranty, express or implied, or assumes any liability or responsibility for the use of this information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suprisingly fun to read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: while torture porn isn't really my thing, parts of this article catered to my stupid sense of humor so well (eg the staff names, the fucking picture of 682, the fact that dr. bright was included in the mzl) that i had to upvote it. the entire concept is grim but also ridiculous enough to make me laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i was so invested in the article that it actually made me confused for a hot second

item.title: Dr. Lang
item.content:encoded:
The Quote
The Quote

A doctor appears from off-screen. Her face is heavily bandaged, though the bandages are clearly being soaked through by dark red and yellow patches of fluid. Only one bloodshot eye is visible from beneath the coverings. She hands Dr. Oldman what appears to be a hydraulic speculum. Dr. Oldman nods.

Dr. Oldman: [Yes, this will do lovely. Dr. Anna Lang, everyone. Isn't she great? (Pauses) Yes, not as great as you, Red. Anyway, throughout this seminar, you'll learn about the various pain centers of the human body, the psychology of horror and torment, and the steps you can take to increase your efficacy in this holiest of efforts.]


I don't know why, but this part stood out to me. I knew this had to be a reference to something, but I couldn't figure out to what. My best guess was the Dr. Anna Lang of SCP-3001 fame.
Spoilers?
An Aside

She's the co-inventor of the Lang-Scranton theory, and her husband is the guy who got sucked in to SCP-3001's wormhole.

For those of you like me who are also curious about this character, read: SCP-3001, Until Death, and then Guess Who's Coming to Dinner? because screw everything else. *shudders*

Like seriously, Guess Who's Coming to Dinner? is my canon forever now.

I think I'm the first to comment on this? That certainly seems to be the case. I don't get the connection to Oldman, though. I also do not seem to understand why she had that specific a fate in this joke SCP article. It felt very detailed to me; that's just my observation or whatever.

This whole thing was wack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is the first time a username/title/whatever thing genuinely caught me off-guard. all i can say is, i wonder what other MZL articles would look like. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this doesn't really seem like a J class…
I know it certainly doesn't fit the bill for a official entry but it certainly doesn't feel all that entirely funny. You had a pretty good opportunity for a funny role change, seeing the SCPs act like humans and vice versa, but it seems you just kinda blew it on torture porn. When you filter out the shock factor, there doesn't seem much all that "humorous"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that this would've been even better if the Assistant Director of Pain and Suffering had been named Radical Doctor Larry Sranton Oldman.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can check code-related stuff like this by scrolling to the bottom of the page, clicking "+ Options", clicking "Page Source", and scrolling to the relevant section.

In this case, the code is the following:

+ show block
– hide block
 [[module ListUsers users="."]] //OverBobble-1 stands up and walks towards the camera. He stops in front of it and smiles.// **OverBobble-1:** Don't think you're getting out of this so easily, %%title%%. We're one big family here at the MZL - and that means everybody gets involved. Everybody. (//Laughs//) We'll see you soon. [[/module]]

It's a pretty standard usage of the ListUsers module and you can read more about them here. I know a few other articles have used it to similar effect, namely SCP-902, SCP-TTKU-J, and the WJS Proposal, if you're interested. The text inside the module isn't visible to guests, so if you're not logged in you won't see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit, the last part petrified me. How did you do that? As far as I know, there is no code to change the name on an article. When i saw my name, man, did I get the shock of my life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is somehow both humorous and very not humorous simultaneously. You’ve got stuff like “Well well well, how the turntables” and the atrocious Bobbleized Black Highlighter cohabiting an article with raw lines like “Remember: the funniest about people who can die in the dark… They can die pretty fucking easily in the light, too.” +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh "how the tables have turned" truly indeed. Would obviously love it if the sister site made mock documentaries of the various human worlds.

+1!

item.title: Image use
item.content:encoded: One of your images is based on the original image for SCP-106. As far as I am aware, said image is of unknown origin and thus possibly copyrighted. Might I suggest use of a different image, perhaps derived from the current image for 106?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I typically am not a fan of torture porn.

But god damn this article made me laugh, and the horror and humor played perfectly off each other.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know I think this would really work better as a main series article in some way. I was very interested throughout but I never really laughed and it felt more on the level of the sillier main series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No matter how many times I read it, the Clef section never fails to be deeply, deeply disturbing to me. Good shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The username Javascript never gets old. I love to feel included :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was absolutely fantastic and had me laughing throughout. The theme is also amazing and really adds to the feel of the article.

On a slightly different note though, is this intended to be a tale or a -J SCP? It's tagged as a tale but is included in the Joke SCP hub and in the SCP section on kaktus' author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The hanging of the SCP folks in the board room… my God. Cuts right through to the heart of how callous the Foundation can be portrayed as, and throws it back in our faces. Absolutely brilliant.

I'm also not sure about the GAW treatment, but I am happy to see that their stature as a GOI warranted inclusion.

And I have to admit, by the end, I was so wrapped up in the story that the username drop actually startled me.

Wonderfully done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would love to arrange a reading of this with the authors or appropriate users voicing individual characters in the dialogue sequences. However, it would be difficult to arrange, certainly. Not least of all because splicing recorded audio from several different sources into a single audio file is something I am inept at.

item.title: Re: BLESS THIS NEW THEME
item.content:encoded:
You broke my computer with your theme, I hate you
Wow! I like it!

item.title: BLESS THIS NEW THEME
item.content:encoded: AND BLESS WOEDENAZ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Anderson built a purification robot. Okay.

Damn this one was a ride.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit to doing a double-take when it somehow read my username at the end.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm… that -J doesn't make me laugh.

I think he's really disturbing. I was really disturbed by this, and not in the good way. But maybe it was the point, when you write this. A thing that looks funny but who is, in fact, really, really disturbing.

No vote for me.

item.title: Woah
item.content:encoded: My favourite SCP turned into a joke SCP?Hell yeah,Good one as always and if you think about it,it's quite disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did you kill Clef?!

YOU MONSTER.

(Joking).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus christ, how horrifying. This is a good -J, but also managed to freak me out quite a bit. So rare that anything can do both. Congratulations. +1, you fantastic bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels waaaaay too much like fanservice for me. There is everything : the author-characters, the major SCPs everyone is almost forced to insta-like if they encounter them in a somewhat correct crosslinking, the new trends (dado)… Feels akward to read an article which tries way too much to hit your "fan soft spot".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah at most there's a detante.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not just you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's not occasionally or slightly extended, that's always and constant duration (of always). I mean that's just a different page CSS (like this one has, in fact), but there's nothing animated about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think they are referring to the pages, mostly by Hippo, that have the CSS module of the pride flag in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The MZL, human in bars, logo? Is this a thing that doesn't work on mobile?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that why the logo keeps occasionally exhibiting a (slightly extended) pride flag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's one hell of a punch line, pretty clever bit of javascript hurling or whatever, in fact I'm curious enough about what happens if you're not logged in I'm going to deliberately log out and reload the page.

Edit: aw, it just cuts off the last couple of paragraphs. Oh well.

Also, wtf is with the dickbottle thing? we do not talk about bottle dick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it's a joke! Why wouldn't it go all out? I believe it can get away with some things here.

That and it's a Freaky Friday thing, what do you THINK SCP's would do with that kind of power? Of course torture of the personel would happen! I'd be surprised if they DIDN'T do that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gonna sound like the grinch of fun here, but I honestly couldn't even get a single chuckle from this article. I know it's a meme to make this comparison but it KINDA feels a bit lolFoundation to me.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It started off funny, the images are hilarious, but the farther you read the more… disturbing it became. Still, its a great article and it's quite clear that a lot of time and effort went into this! So, +1

Edit: First time I read this I was NOT a member, once I was I came right here and commented my admiration. Only now did I re-read it AS a member and the ending mentioning the username part scared me for a sec…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GAW seems pretty into survival, though, and probably not super inclined to stick their necks out to save the Foundation from whatever karmic justice this is. "Yeah cool i guess whatever" seems like the a perfect response for them and is probably close enough to on-board for Bobble to put it that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, GAW is generally not in favour of anything that does any real harm or death from what I see. They'd be down for the Foundation being radically restructured, but not into retributive violence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic article, but I strongly object to what you implied about Sousa marches.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Around May, some people who disagreed with the mods' handling of Pride Month broke off and created their own SCP-like site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's got this comical air that has some tonal dissonance with the long stretch of basically torture porn, followed by, what sounded like, a decidedly mean spirited jab at RPC completely out of left field.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really liked this +1

But this is my first time hearing of the RPC. Can someone tell me what in the hecc that is? (Or rather, why that is)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bobble IS torture porn. Bobble was literally MADE as torture porn. Without torture porn you basically have no Bobble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ples answer res of questiens i'd apreciat it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how u fit eel in staffroom

Carefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what in the shit
i have not even read this and i can tell that oh holy heckin' golly gee gosh it's gonna be a doozy
kak what have you done
also you listen to johnny cash too i thought only edgenerds like me listened to cash

after reading: it was everything i wanted it to be and more, but i have a few concerns
682 no care about pain, he only care about kill
y are good/neutral anomalies- dado, brite, spanko- also working for the mzl
y u use the names of the authors and not avatars: "roget" is "rosen" and "lament" is "kondraki"
y anna not ded
y bobl of all people got control
y robrt not robrt and insteed scranton or is bobble just fukken with us
y is bobble freaked out by dunderson's kiddie killer bot
how u fit eel in staffroom
is gorber gar president of the united sa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THIS IS IT, EVERYONE ELSE CAN GO HOME.

Okay, but in all seriousness though, this is fucking great and I'm actually kinda curious to see if this will be continued in any way, storyline wise. Such as, is there a resistance? What happened to the normal D-Class? Are they in partial control? I mean, they were also victim to the Foundation. Also, not sure why, but that last thing with the name script really gave me a chill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this one really weird sect of defected, socially stunted ex-Foundation researchers and their nutritionally deficient boy leader who apparently really hate gay people. They just seemed thrilled to be noticed by literally anyone at all.

This paragraph extended my lifespan by at least 3 years and cured my depression.

+++1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, I don't think the article would be funny without the torture porn because, from what I can gather, this is a parody of people who don't handle sensitive subjects such as torture with the appropriate amount of care. It wouldn't be funny without the torture porn because the torture porn is what I believe I'm meant to find funny.

Also, I understand that, were this to happen, the anomalies would definitely torture the staff. Doesn't mean I have to like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this one really weird sect of defected, socially stunted ex-Foundation researchers and their nutritionally deficient boy leader who apparently really hate gay people. They just seemed thrilled to be noticed by literally anyone at all.

who apparently really hate gay people.

Well then. Would have liked it more if you went further on the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like others have said, the way GAW was presented here seems rather out of character. The rest of this was rather humorous with a few exceptions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you really think that the humor would have held up without the "torture porn"? I don't. In fact, if I try to imagine this same article being written without the torture it seems somewhat lifeless. It is the contrast between the different elements that really makes this article work.

Besides, if this situation occurred, and the ones in charge had the same mindset, they *would* be casually torturing and killing the staff. It wouldn't make sense for them not to.

The torture porn makes sense both in-universe and out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is wonderful! Even if there are a couple parts here and there that fell flat, the whole is greater than the sum of its parts.

The humor doesn't take away from the horror. On the contrary, it is what makes the horror work at all. I especially love the edited pictures and silly names of the new doctors and researchers.

The bit at the end might have been a bit corny, but I enjoyed it anyway. I cant really explain why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely hilarious, no equivocation. One of the funniest things on this entire site, and insanely well-written.
